title: p63 supports aerobic respiration through hexokinase II
author_string: Viticchiè, Guiditta, Agostini, Massimiliano, Lena, Anna Maria, Mancini, Mara, Zhou, Huiqing et al.
journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences
publisher: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences
abstract:
Significance
          The importance of ΔNp63 in controlling metabolism has not been investigated so far. We identified a regulatory pathway involving ΔNp63 and the glycolytic enzyme hexokinase 2 (HK2). ΔNp63 direct-dependent regulation of HK2 expression contributes to the coupling between glucose metabolism and oxidative phosphorylation, providing the energy necessary to sustain cell proliferation and protecting cells from oxidative stress. The ΔNp63–HK2axis is also present in epithelial cancer cells, suggesting that ΔNp63 could participate in cancer metabolic reprogramming.
